Online Retail Analysis — Full Project Conclusion

Dataset: UCI Online Retail II  |  Records: 525,461 transactions  |  Period: Dec 2009 – Dec 2010

1. Data Quality Assessment & Cleaning

The raw dataset contained significant quality issues requiring systematic cleaning:

Figure 1: Data quality issues — missing values and invalid records

The cleaning pipeline processed data through 5 sequential stages:

Figure 2: Data cleaning pipeline — 525K rows reduced to 349K (66.4% retention)

1.1 Box Plot Analysis (Before Cleaning)

Initial distributions revealed extreme outliers — prices from -£53,594 to £25,111:

Figure 3: Price & Quantity distributions before cleaning

ColumnOutlier CountPercentage
Quantity57,87011.01%
Price35,2736.71%
Customer ID00.00%

2. Key Performance Indicators

Figure 4: KPI Dashboard after data cleaning

KPIValue
Total Revenue£4,506,747
Unique Customers4,140
Unique Products3,644
Total Transactions17,216
Avg Transaction Value£261.78
Avg Items per Transaction144.29
Key Insight: Average transaction value of £261.78 and 144 items/transaction confirm this is a wholesale/trade business, not typical B2C.

Figure 12: UK dominates at 65–70% of revenue

Risk: Over-reliance on UK. Opportunity: EU expansion (NL, DE, FR) could add £450K–£810K annually.

6. Customer Segmentation

4 tiers by lifetime spending: Occasional (£0–100), Regular (£100–500), Premium (£500–1K), VIP (£1K+).

6.1 Segment Distribution

Figure 17: VIP = 30.1% of customers

6.2 Revenue by Segment

Figure 18: VIP generates £3.4M — 76.2% of total

6.3 Revenue vs Customer %

Figure 19: The Pareto gap — 30% of customers drive 76% of revenue

8. Strategic Recommendations

Figure 30: Projected £1.8M annual revenue impact (+40% growth)

InitiativeQuick Win6-Month12-Month
VIP Retention (+3%)£29K£87K£175K
Win-Back Campaign£7K£34K£68K
Cross-Sell Engine—£275K£551K
Pricing Optimization—£90K£180K
International Expansion—£112K£810K
Inventory Optimization—£22K£45K
TOTAL£36K£620K£1,829K

9. Final Conclusion

This business lives and dies by its VIP customers. 30% of customers drive 76% of revenue.

Top 3 actions:

  1. Protect VIP revenue — loyalty programs and proactive retention (prevents £172K+ loss)
  2. Implement cross-selling — purchase-pattern recommendations (adds £551K/year)
  3. Expand internationally — Netherlands, Germany, France (adds £810K/year)

Combined: £1.8M additional annual revenue — a 40% improvement over the £4.5M baseline.

UCI Online Retail II (2009–2010) | 525,461 transactions | 348,613 after cleaning | 30 visualizations
